name: oomkill-increase-memory-limits
description: OOMKilled pod: increase container memory limits and requests to prevent recurrence
steps: Identify the affected deployment via: kubectl get pod <name> -n <namespace> -o yaml
Get current memory limits: kubectl get deployment <name> -n <namespace> -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[*].resources}'
Calculate new limits: double current limit, cap at 80% of node allocatable memory
Patch deployment resources via MCP tool: patch_deployment_resources
Wait for rollout: kubectl rollout status deployment/<name> -n <namespace> --timeout=60s
Verify pod is Running: kubectl get pods -n <namespace> -l app=<name>
Monitor for 5 minutes: watch kubectl top pods -n <namespace>
Update GitOps repo with new resource values for permanence
